prompt: A generic medical-product instruction illustration in simple black line-art on a pure white background, in the universal patient-information-leaflet style. Subject: a pair of cupped human hands held just under a wall-mounted or counter-top automatic soap / hand-sanitizer dispenser. The dispenser is a simple rectangular container with a small nozzle pointing downward; a few small droplet shapes fall from the nozzle into the cupped palms below. Hands shown from a three-quarter front angle, fingers together, palms cupped to receive the liquid. Style: uniform medium-weight black ink lines on clean white, no color anywhere. Minimal shading — only a few short parallel hatching lines under the fingers and inside the dispenser to suggest soft shadow / volume; the rest is empty white. No frame, no border, no caption text, no labels, no logos, no numbers, no arrows, no decorative elements. Subject centered in a 1:1 square composition with generous clean white margins. The line work is confident but slightly imperfect — drawn-by-hand feel — like a generic patient information leaflet illustration found across many medical-product packagings.
